verdict_reasoning: ATP5F1B encodes the beta subunit of the mitochondrial ATP synthase (Complex V). Its primary localization is the inner mitochondrial membrane/matrix face, where it forms the F1 catalytic head. However, there is documented evidence that ATP synthase subunits, including the beta subunit, can appear on the plasma membrane surface of certain cell types (endothelial cells, cancer cells, hepatocytes) where they function as ecto-ATP synthase or angiostatin-binding sites. This surface form is cell-type restricted and not the dominant localization, fitting 'contextual / tissue_restricted_surface'.
